title: Phyllorkis cylindracea
taxonomy: Phyllorkis cylindracea is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Phyllorkis and is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae. Phyllorkis cylindracea is also a member of the tribe Coelogyninae within the orchid family. This classification indicates its relationship to other orchids and helps to identify its distinct characteristics. The classification of Phyllorkis cylindracea is subject to ongoing research and revision in the field of botany.

intro: Phyllorkis cylindracea, also known as Cystorchis cylindracea, is a species of orchid native to tropical Africa, characterized by its cylindrical pseudobulb and small, white flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against infections and oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Phyllorkis cylindracea include glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional medicine and is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being used in the production of traditional remedies and folk medicines.
